content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 16, 1989 
Ross S. Clark 
Principal Consultant 
Office of Assemblyrnember Maxine Waters 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ross: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-101 
This will confirm the advice you outlined in your 
February 9 letter to me. 
Your statement of my advice is 
accurate. 
Nice to hear from you. 
Sincerely, 
'~)~/ (- t~. 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
DMG:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


WATERS 
CAUCUS CHAIRWOMAN 
Diane Griffiths, General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
428 J street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Diane: 
February 9, 1989 
COMMITTEES 
CHAF~WOMAN 
ON 
ADMINISTRATION 
Would you be kind enough to confirm your telephone advice of 
February 2, 1989 to Ms. Barbara Milman. 
It is my understanding that Ms. Milman explained that 
Democratic Members pay for their caucus lunches annually, and in 
advance. 
the question asked concerned any FPPC rules or 
objections to the establishment of a lunch account the sole 
purpose of which would be to deposit members checks and pay the 
lunch caterers. 
This account would be administered by a staff 
member and not an elected Member. 
It is my further understanding that you advised that such an 
account was permissible. 
Thank you in advance for your 
RSCjrj 
confirmation, and best 
Ross ,f- Clark 
V:oAA 
princ~ple Consultant 
\ 
\ 
regards, 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 15, 1989 
Ross S. Clark 
Principle Consultant 
Assemblywoman Maxine Waters 
state Capitol 
P.O. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Re: 
File No. 89-101 
Dear Mr. Clark: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on February 14, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you 
as to the information needed. 
If your request is for informal 
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See 
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
r\ 
V, 
Diane M. 
Griffiths 
General Counsel 
(L 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.question: Would it violate FPPC rules or policies to establish a lunch account for Democratic Members caucus lunches, where the sole purpose would be to deposit members' checks and pay lunch caterers, with the account administered by a staff member rather than an elected Member?
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether the FPPC permits establishment of a dedicated lunch account funded by Democratic Members' advance annual payments, administered by staff, to pay caucus lunch caterers.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that establishing a lunch account funded by member contributions and administered by staff to pay for caucus lunches does not violate FPPC rules.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three letters: (1) incoming request from Ross Clark dated Feb 9 seeking confirmation of phone advice; (2) FPPC acknowledgment letter dated Feb 15 confirming receipt; (3) FPPC response letter dated Feb 16 confirming the prior advice was accurate. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present, but the substantive legal question and answer are clearly stated in the incoming request and confirmed in the response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would it violate FPPC rules or policies to establish a lunch account for Democratic Members caucus lunches, where the sole purpose would be to deposit members' checks and pay lunch caterers, with the account administered by a staff member rather than an elected Member?

CONCLUSION: Such an account was permissible.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed its prior telephone advice that Democratic Members may establish a lunch account funded by annual member contributions and administered by staff to pay for caucus lunches. This follows up on informal advice provided on February 2, 1989.
